programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the pressure from Mel rising, Sharon suggests going for lunch with Keanu and Louise to talk but is frustrated when Phil invites himself along. Ben tries to impress Jason, one of Phil's business associates, by telling him he can get him a good deal on a fancy car. Ted and Wanda's second date ends in disaster, Rainie loses control after Ian confronts her about her drug-taking and Gray tells Chantelle he's going to see a therapist about his behaviour(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Evan shows no sign of giving up and pursues Chloe to a cottage in the countryside, where his obsession with her takes a violent turn that leaves her in fear for her life. Cameron receives bad news that forces him to abandon his efforts to help Ric, who has finally been forced to acknowledge his deteriorating health(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Rachel finally comes face to face with Shaun, who offers up the location of the CIA safe house before managing to evade arrest once more. The fugitive soldier flees into an encounter with two mysterious strangers, who offer to provide him with the answers he is looking for. Rachel pays Napier a visit, and the meeting leaves her more convinced than ever that the CCTV network has been compromised(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Documentary following six junior doctors beginning new placements at Salford Royal hospital. A doctor assigned to the geriatrics ward has to work on his bedside manner, while a colleague in the colorectal ward has her knowledge of bowel surgery put to the test and is given the difficult task of delivering some bad news to a patient(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. One team has to master two vegetarian dishes, while the other team takes on a fava bean fallafel with taboulleh and flatbreads. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at The Metropolitan Police Training Academy in Hendon, North London(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue Perkins continues on her trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. In the concluding episode, Sue travels to the ancient city of Kyoto to spend time with geishas and meets those working hard to keep geisha culture alive. She then heads south of Kyoto to experience free diving with Ama divers, before travelling to Hiroshima, where she meets Mr Tetsushi, an 84-year-old survivor of the atomic blast during the Second World War. Finally, Sue returns to Tokyo, to see how the Japanese are tackling challenges of the future(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at life in the In The Style offices in Greater Manchester and the new world of ultra-fast fashion. The series follows the work of 32-year-old business owner Adam Frisby and his team during the summer season, as the company competes with rival fast fashion brands to sign up the latest talent, come up with the hottest styles and supply them faster and better than any other brand. In the first episode, social media influencer Lorna Luxe launches her new summer range and has big plans for a glamorous shoot(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane and Arron Crascall go on adventures around the world, using only a smartphone to gain the vital information they need to survive. In the first episode, they cross the Sahara, where they face challenges including making a fire with camel dung, cooking a lizard while it is still alive, and surviving an encounter with a spitting cobra(n)

Wanda gives Ted another chance and the pair have a heart-to-heart about Joyce(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Matt Allwright and Nikki Fox compare cost and customer service at the nation's biggest supermarkets, and a new Swab Mob investigation reveals hygiene failings at an iconic British institution. An airport parking company taking its customers for a ride gets a surprise of its own in Rogue Traders and Helen Skelton exposes the household staple causing chaos because of the way the packet says we should get rid of it(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the country lanes of Northern Ireland in their Rolls-Royce. Angus has designs on a piece of Victorian furniture while Roo hits the dance floor(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Hook, Hampshire to help young couple Cat and Joel. The newlyweds dream of a family garden for the children that they plan to have - a space full of grass and wildlife friendly flowers. However, all they have now is a concrete garden made up of nothing but paving stones(n)

programme.title: Transplant Games

programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales, where they talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table - in a local pub. In the evening, Paul organises some entertainment, while Bob struggles to cook a heart healthy dinner to round off their trip(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team goes island-hopping around the Western Isles and out to Shetland. Nick Crane hunts for gannets on Eriskay, and Neil Oliver hears the tragic story of a 1918 shipwreck off the Isle of Lewis in which more than 200 servicemen returning from the First World War were drowned. Plus, Miranda Krestovnikoff searches for otters which struggle to survive on the wild coast, and Tessa Dunlop investigates how the epic voyage of HMS Challenger in the 19th century first revealed the secrets of life in the depths of the world's oceans(n)

The Netherlands dominated this event last year, with Annemiek van Vleuten leading home a Dutch one-two-three(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Powderham Castle in Devon, antiques experts Will Axon and Mark Stacey find several treasures to take off to auction, including a shotgun case that belonged to a First World War hero and a pristine collection of Dinky toys. Plus, Paul Martin delves into the past of the castle, which has been home to the same family for more than 600 years, and uncovers a scandal that changed the course of that family's history(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.desc: Armed with his Edwardian Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo conducts important research in an historic tea room, built by an Edwardian immigrant to the city of York. In Leeds he discovers the city's textile heritage, and also investigates Bradford's musical heritage(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which famous faces compete in the cross-country bargain-hunting challenge. TV presenters Denise Van Outen and Kelly Brook set out on a trip around south-east England in classic cars, receiving expert guidance from Charles Hanson and Tim Medhurst as they visit antique shops and emporiums to find items to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A crew of RNLI volunteers in Newquay is called out to a shipwreck off the Cornish coast, where a diver who has lost his mouthpiece needs rescuing from 30 metres underwater. A yacht in the Dover Strait starts to sink - and may collide with a cargo ship before going under - and a Dorset crew search for a windsurfer who has got caught in a storm(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Katy picks her four favourite letter writers to meet face-to-face at a speed dating event in London, including 74-year-old former professional footballer Carl, and painter and decorator John. Meanwhile, it's decision time for dairy farmers Martin and Grace, who have each invited three potential suitors back to their farms. Both are looking for a partner that will fall in love with not only them, but also their rural lifestyle and are hoping sparks will fly with at least one of their guests during their weekend stay(n)

programme.title: Defending the Guilty
programme.desc: As Will grapples with whether to tell his girlfriend about his recent indiscretion - but he has no such dilemma when in Chambers, as Liam has already told everyone. However, his newfound notoriety does throw up a juicy opportunity from Miles, who offers him the case that everyone wants. Meanwhile, Caroline fears her prospects of becoming a QC are dubious, and wonders how much to let the jury know about a particularly unappealing client. Legal sitcom, starring Will Sharpe and Katherine Parkinson(n)

programme.desc: Jo Coburn presents highlights from the Labour Party autumn conference in Brighton(n)

programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the third round of fixtures, including Kansas City Chiefs v Baltimore Ravens, San Francisco 49ers v Pittsburgh Steelers, and Seattle Seahawks v New Orleans Saints(n)

programme.title: Inside the Vatican
programme.desc: A film crew gains exceptional access to Vatican, afforded a year to examine its inner workings. Cameras capture everyone including Pope Francis himself, his head of security, the nuns who cater for the homeless, the choristers of the Sistine Chapel, the papal gardener and the chief of the diplomatic corps to gain a unique and exclusive insight into the lives of those who live and work in this independent City State. The first of two programmes follows the build-up to Easter, when tens of thousands of pilgrims arrive and Pope Francis takes centre stage in the world's biggest theatre of faith(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Dramatised documentary examining how Hitler and the Nazi party rose to power in Germany during the 1930s, revealing the greed, manipulation and frightening underestimation of Adolf Hitler's ambition. In 1930, Germany was a liberal democracy, with elections, parliament and the rule of law, but just four years later its leader was a dictator and its government is in the hands of murderers. Leading historians and experts take viewers inside the minds of those hellbent on gaining power and those who tried to stop them(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life

Cameras follow puffins, diving gannets and storm petrels nesting in an ancient Pictish tower, a mother otter protecting her young twin cubs, and capture a pod of orcas hunting seals of the island's rocky coasts(n)

Items range from an art deco pair of Lalique vases to a 9.5mm projector. Presenter Paul Martin also visits Powis Castle in mid-Wales and finds out about their collection of Indian artefacts(n)

Then, Michael heads south into Nottinghamshire and to the former pit village of Eastwood, before arriving in Nantwich, where he takes a dip in the briny(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Painting conservator Lucia Scalisi faces a serious challenge when presented with a perforated painting by celebrated British artist Fred Appleyard(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As the semi-finals get under way, the remaining three contestants go head to head for the first time as they are challenged to redesign an entire hair salon, with industry experts Kelly Hoppen, Sophie Robinson and Abigail Ahern on hand to offer the designers their advice. With just two days to complete this ambitious challenge, the three designers head to sunny Whitstable on the Kent coast to visit the salons for the first time. One designer decides to go for an ambitious theme with sophisticated lighting scheme, while another opts for bold colours and brave design choices. In the third salon, the competitor decides to play it safe, but will their salon have enough ambition to impress the judges and earn a place in the final? Judging the finished salons alongside Michelle Ogundehin this week is design legend, Lawrence Llewelyn-Bowen(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: In a radical social experiment based on a scheme in Germany, 14 young volunteers between the ages of 18 and 24 spend four weeks delivering patient care on the NHS frontline at Royal Derby Hospital, aiming to find out whether volunteers could help relieve the pressures of staff on the wards and make a positive difference to patient care in the NHS. As the volunteers embark on their final week of the scheme, The Royal Derby Hospital senior staff assess the success of the experiment. Meanwhile, Michael struggles to perform personal care for patients in the elderly care ward, and the volunteers' personal prejudices are challenged when a homeless patient who is suffering from alcoholism is brought into the hospital(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Wonderbra model Eva Herzigova recalls the year male motorists swerved left, right and centre at the sight of her billboard posters, while Oasis and Blur released albums Definitely Maybe and Parklife, setting the scene for future rivalry. The nation's hairdressers found themselves inundated with Jennifer Aniston haircut requests as Friends hit TV screens, faith in the British film industry was restored as Four Weddings and a Funeral became a box-office smash and John Travolta starred in Quentin Tarantino's Pulp Fiction(n)

programme.title: Nomad: In the Footsteps of Bruce Chatwin
programme.desc: Documentary in which Werner Herzog sets out on a journey inspired by his friend, writer and adventurer Bruce Chatwin, who had a passion for the nomadic life. Carrying the rucksack that Chatwin gave him on his deathbed, Herzog travels to South America, Australia and the Welsh borders, uncovering stories of lost tribes, wanderers and dreamers(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary examines how President Xi Jinping has made China a more assertive presence on the world stage since his accession in 2013. For decades, Beijing followed a policy of 'hide your strength and bide your time', but that has changed - creating alarm in the West over China's growing influence. As Xi faces his greatest test yet - a confrontation in Hong Kong - how will the newly resurgent China react?(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: Investigating conspiracy theories surrounding multi-billionaire George Soros and the allegations of his secret plots to overthrow governments and flood countries with migrants(n)

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Dover Castle for a special edition first aired to mark 80 years since the start of the Second World War, bringing insights into the conflict through personal stories and family mementos. A photo album belonging to an Austrian soldier offers a previously unseen glimpse of Neville Chamberlain's ill-fated meeting with Adolf Hitler in 1938, while Fiona Bruce meets 95-year-old Ray Palmer, who has the rare distinction of being both a child evacuee and then a serving soldier(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The search for a Kent woman's relatives runs into difficulties on her father's side of the family. The team uncovers the story of a naval hero during a case in Wales(n)

programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history during the prehistoric, Roman, Saxon and Tudor eras(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Food writer Mary Berry is one of Britain's best-loved TV cooks, finding fame thanks to her judging role on The Great British Bake Off. A self-confessed go-getter, she wants to trace her family tree to know where she gets her energy and drive from. The journey begins in Bath, where Mary grew up, and takes her to Norwich, where both her grandparents lived. Along the way she uncovers ancestors including a bankrupt book-binder, a self-motivated corset-maker and a third who worked in a much more familiar profession(n)

Along the way he discovers a childhood favourite - a miniature tank engine with three carriages in dark red - made by Frank Hornby, and learns the story of a Welsh countess who lived in Gwrych Castle. In Snowdonia, he also rides the waves with a Welsh surfing champion(n)

programme.title: MOTDx

programme.title: Jessica Ennis-Hill's World Class of 2019
programme.desc: The former athlete looks ahead to the 17th staging of the World Athletics Championships, which this year take place in Doha, Qatar. The presenter, a three-time gold medallist in these games herself, talks to British contenders Dina Asher-Smith and Katarina Johnson-Thompson, and Norwegian middle-distance brothers Henrik and Jakob Ingebrigtsen(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: The third leg of Dave Myers and Si King's trip sees them on the trail of Oklahoma's famous beef. In Oklahoma City, a classic T-bone steak is followed by a visit to the city's Vietnamese community, where they knock up their national dish pho - a broth made of beef or chicken. After checking out El Reno's unique onion burger the duo hook up with a couple of fire crews who like to cook - especially brisket. An organic sweet potato farm inspires Dave and Si to make sweet potato brownies, before they finish this stretch on a cattle ranch with America's most iconic custodian of the land - the cowboy(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: As Britain loses its measles-free status and cases surge worldwide, the World Health Organisation has labelled 'vaccine hesitancy' a top 10 threat to global health. But campaigners claim that vaccines are unsafe, and the cause of a health catastrophe. Decades of scientific research do not back them up. Yet their ideas not only persist - they are reaching around the globe. This documentary examines the arguments of those who campaign against vaccines, and hears from people at the heart of alleged conspiracies. And it analyses how vaccine-sceptic ideas are spreading, what gives them currency and who may be benefiting(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As the semi-finals get underway, the remaining three contestants go head to head for the first time as they are challenged to redesign an entire hair salon, with industry experts Kelly Hoppen, Sophie Robinson and Abigail Ahern on hand to offer the designers their advice. With just two days to complete this ambitious challenge, the three designers head to sunny Whitstable on the Kent coast to visit the salons for the first time. One designer decides to go for an ambitious theme with sophisticated lighting scheme, while another opts for bold colours and brave design choices. In the third salon, the competitor decides to play it safe, but will their salon have enough ambition to impress the judges and earn a place in the final? Judging the finished salons alongside Michelle Ogundehin this week is design legend, Lawrence Llewelyn- Bowen(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: During the busy summer season a fox family plays below the Carmarthen Fans, lizards bask in the sun on limestone pavements in the upper Swansea valley and hundreds of dragonflies emerge from pools in the uplands near Brecon. Iolo Williams heads to the Black Mountain foothills as sheep are gathered by shepherds on horseback and some dedicated volunteers try to repair a mountain. Ancient botanical cures for ailments and old steam railways are two of many hidden histories(n)

programme.title: England's Forgotten Queen: The Life and Death of Lady Jane Grey
programme.desc: Three days into her reign, the clock is already ticking for Jane as Mary Tudor, determined to seize power of the throne, raises an army. The Duke of Northumberland is sent from the Tower of London to lead Jane's forces against Mary, and heavily armed ships are deployed to prevent her escaping by sea. With both armies matched in size and firepower, the future of the country hangs in the balance(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Jennifer O'Leary examines the impact of Margaret Thatcher's election on the conflict, hearing a shocking admission about the Brighton bomb which almost killed the prime minister and talking to individuals who were involved in smuggling weapons for the IRA. Plus, how Gerry Adams was elected to parliament led to rising tension between the political and paramilitary wings of the Republican movement(n)

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose tells the story of Captain James Cook's historic 1768 voyage to find a vast southern continent. No such land mass was discovered, but the crew of the Endeavour went on to Tahiti and Australia, which Cook claimed for Britain. The ship was almost ripped apart on the Great Barrier Reef, but the captain's navigational skills eventually steered it back home to safety(n)

programme.title: The Last Explorers
programme.desc: Neil Oliver charts the story of naturalist William Speirs Bruce, who set out to conquer Antarctica in the early 20th century in the cause of scientific advancement rather than national pride(n)

programme.title: Inside the Perfect Predator
programme.desc: Documentary using ground-breaking computer graphics and close-up photography to examine four of the natural world's most extraordinary hunters. The programme follows a peregrine falcon on a mission to kill for its chicks, a great white shark hungry for seal blubber, a cheetah struggling to provide for its cubs and a Nile crocodile poised to ambush its dinner(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Shelbys are lured by the Italians into a cat-and-mouse chase on the street of Birmingham, where it becomes clear that Tommy has met his match. Tommy tries to console himself with a visit from an old flame, but it soon becomes clear that he cannot always get what he wants. As his factory lies idle, Tommy confronts the possibility that the Communists might win and he'll be deemed a traitor to his class. Meanwhile, Changretta prepares to spring another trap. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy finds himself engaged in bloody battle with Luca Changretta and his gang. The family gather to find out what happened, but Lizzie has even greater news to break. Meanwhile, an army colonel has questions for Ada about her past as a Communist, and Jessie Eden confirms just how far she is prepared to go in pursuit of her cause. Sensing an opportunity to capitalise on his situation, Luca Changretta makes his way to London to present a plan to Alfie Solomons. Starring Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Adrien Brody(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The night of the big fight arrives, and as the bell rings and the crowd goes wild, dangers lurk in the shadows for Tommy Shelby and his family, and Tommy soon discovers who he can truly trust. Meanwhile, as Changretta plays his final ace, he sets in motion a series of events that will change the Peaky Blinders forever. Starring Cillian Murphy, Adrien Brody, Tom Hardy, Helen McCrory, Charlie Murphy, Sophie Rundle, Paul Anderson, Kate Phillips, Ned Dennehy and Jack Rowan(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: Iolo Williams finds himself in the midst of a flock of thousands of fieldfares arriving from Europe to escape the colder continent, with the birds gorging themselves on berries in trees surrounding the smallest church in Wales. In the Usk Valley, bats feed before they hibernate in caves and migrating ducks gather on Talybont Reservoir, while sea trout are heading upstream to spawn(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian heads out to sea to hear the story of the Empire Heritage, a cargo ship filled with Sherman tanks sunk off Malin Head during the Battle of the Atlantic in 1944. He gets the opportunity to fly a Second World War aircraft while visiting an RAF flying boat base on Lough Erne, and meets the experts intent on discovering the identities of the remains of soldiers killed during the conflict(n)

programme.title: Churchill and the Movie Mogul
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the friendship and collaboration between Winston Churchill and the film producer Alexander Korda. In 1934, Korda signed Churchill up as a screenwriter and historical advisor, and Churchill provided script notes for Korda's productions and penned a screenplay. When the Second World War broke out, Korda was sent on a mission to Hollywood to help bring America into the war, with positive results(n)

programme.title: Orson Welles Over Europe
programme.desc: Simon Callow tells the story of actor and director Orson Welles' self-imposed exile in Europe, which saw him star in The Third Man before immersing himself in challenging work on stage and screen - as well as developing a passion for bull-fighting(n)

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver tells the story of how the age of the Vikings came to an end, using archaeological evidence to explore the lives of the first Danish kings, the spread of Christianity in Scandinavia, and the voyages of discovery undertaken by Norse sailors in the Atlantic. He also profiles Canute, the former king of England, and reveals why the reputation he has accrued over the past millennium does a disservice to his diplomatic feats(n)

programme.title: Stories from the Dark Earth: Meet the Ancestors Revisited
programme.desc: Archaeologist Julian Richards revisits some of his most important digs to discover how science, conservation and new finds have changed people's understanding of ancient history. His journey continues at the excavation of two burials from the Stone Age - the grave of a Neolithic family in Dorset, and a tomb in Orkney that is helping to reveal some unexpected burial rites from more than 5,000 years ago(n)

programme.title: Bacchus Uncovered: Ancient God of Ecstasy
programme.desc: Professor Bettany Hughes investigates the story of Bacchus, travelling to Georgia, Britain, Jordan and Greece to discover his origins and his presence in the modern world(n)

programme.title: Nelson's Caribbean Hell-Hole: An Eighteenth Century Navy Graveyard Uncovered
programme.desc: After the discovery of human bones on a beach in Antigua, historian Sam Willis investigates one of the darkest chapters of Britain's imperial past. As archaeologists excavate a mass grave of British soldiers, he explores the island's ruins and discovers how the sugar islands of the Caribbean were rife with sun, sea, war, tropical diseases and poisoned rum(n)

programme.title: Churchill and the Movie Mogul
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the friendship and collaboration between Winston Churchill and the film producer Alexander Korda. When the Second World War broke out, Korda was sent on a mission to Hollywood to help bring America into the war, with positive results(n)

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part series in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. Along the way he meets local people including gun-toting priests and the last sponge-divers in the world, and discovers how they are coping with their country's current crises(n)

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story(n)

programme.title: Land of the Lost Wolves
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary following the lives of a wolf pack in North America, wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan and the team discover most of the animals have been killed by locals. However, as they try to find out whether any of the predators survived, there is a glimmer of hope on the horizon(n)

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Greengrocer Yusuf finds a little star in a delivery from outer space. The star is very sad, so Yusuf and the people of Biggleton do their best to cheer it up. Narrated by Eamonn Holmes(n)

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: At the end of a busy day, E just wants to sing himself to sleep - but Little Bo Peep and friends have other plans(n)

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘teddy' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure(n)

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Learn all about the number 3 with Numberblock Three, who arrives with a bang - and a song-and-dance about her favourite number(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny is sad when his new orange balloon bursts, so Flop helps him put the fragments in a memory box, where things too broken to be fixed but too special to throw away are kept(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The fruit tea-loving bird-like characters learn about high and low when they watch a woman trying to hang a mirror at the right height. Back in the boat, the Twirlywoo Screen gives them a see-saw to play with(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a cafe, whilst Mr Tumble and Aunt Polly get ready for the Tumbleton trifle competition(n)

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies become a pop group and perform a pop song. In Tummy Tales, children watch a one man band playing music and accompany him by playing their own music(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear invite babies and their grown-ups to explore gloves through discovery, play, song and story(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy worries about the lift at the department store, but with the help of his friends in the Art World he learns what the buttons are for and how to get safely out again(n)

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: Killian wants somewhere safe to store his money while he saves up for a toy truck, so his mum takes him to a local bank to open his first account.(n)

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 70 million years to meet the miniature magyarosaurus, a relative of the giant sauropod dinosaurs, which grew no bigger than a pony. But when he arrives on their prehistoric island home he has to shrink down as well to hide from a giant flying reptile as tall as a giraffe(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Today's Swashbucklers take on the Coconutty Raft and Pirate Cannons in pursuit of Gem's jewels. The naughty pirates are plagued by weevils and the only way to lure them away is to tempt them with biscuits(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels ask Duggee if they can grow something when he sows seeds, they decide on onions, carrots, cucumber, pumpkins and strawberries(n)

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: The team helps the rabbits find a more peaceful home after a nearby geyer erupts(n)

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy goes to Okido and finds out electricity can lead to music, lights and disco dancing(n)

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie checks out three amazing emergency machines(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Benjamin and Cotton-tail become lost in the Shadowy Woods during a game of peek-a-boo, and Peter tries to help them elude a prowling Mr Tod(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Gail Force struggles to make herself heard over the noise of a drill when reporting from some roadworks, and Keith Fitt is bothered by a troublesome fly in a karate class(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Gramps teaches Waffle to read while Mrs Hobbs is dog-sitting Jelly next door(n)

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: It's raining hard, but the children put on their waterproofs and have a good time outside.(n)

programme.desc: Hugo and his friends encourage young viewers to question the world around them(n)

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Colly Wobble likes to do things differently, including driving her car backwards(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small plant a little plant in Mother's garden. They get impatient for it to grow, but have little idea what they're letting themselves in for. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the pressure from Mel rising, Sharon suggests going for lunch with Keanu and Louise to talk but is frustrated when Phil invites himself along. Ben tries to impress Jason, one of Phil's business associates, by telling him he can get him a good deal on a fancy car. Ted and Wanda's second date ends in disaster, Rainie loses control after Ian confronts her about her drug-taking and Gray tells Chantelle he's going to see a therapist about his behaviour(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Evan shows no sign of giving up and pursues Chloe to a cottage in the countryside, where his obsession with her takes a violent turn that leaves her in fear for her life. Cameron receives bad news that forces him to abandon his efforts to help Ric, who has finally been forced to acknowledge his deteriorating health(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Rachel finally comes face to face with Shaun, who offers up the location of the CIA safe house before managing to evade arrest once more. The fugitive soldier flees into an encounter with two mysterious strangers, who offer to provide him with the answers he is looking for. Rachel pays Napier a visit, and the meeting leaves her more convinced than ever that the CCTV network has been compromised(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Documentary following six junior doctors beginning new placements at Salford Royal hospital. A doctor assigned to the geriatrics ward has to work on his bedside manner, while a colleague in the colorectal ward has her knowledge of bowel surgery put to the test and is given the difficult task of delivering some bad news to a patient(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. One team has to master two vegetarian dishes, while the other team takes on a fava bean fallafel with taboulleh and flatbreads. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at The Metropolitan Police Training Academy in Hendon, North London(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards: Rugby, Apartheid and Me
programme.desc: Fifty years ago, Gareth Edwards captained Wales during a Springbok's tour disrupted by anti-apartheid protests. Now, Gareth meets players and protesters from the controversial rugby tours of 1969 and 1974 and discovers how these events changed South Africa. Later, Gareth travels to see a game in a township Port Elizabeth and witnesses for himself the present day cost of apartheid for millions of people in South Africa(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue Perkins continues on her trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. In the concluding episode, Sue travels to the ancient city of Kyoto to spend time with geishas and meets those working hard to keep geisha culture alive. She then heads south of Kyoto to experience free diving with Ama divers, before travelling to Hiroshima, where she meets Mr Tetsushi, an 84-year-old survivor of the atomic blast during the Second World War. Finally, Sue returns to Tokyo, to see how the Japanese are tackling challenges of the future(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at life in the In The Style offices in Greater Manchester and the new world of ultra-fast fashion. The series follows the work of 32-year-old business owner Adam Frisby and his team during the summer season, as the company competes with rival fast fashion brands to sign up the latest talent, come up with the hottest styles and supply them faster and better than any other brand. In the first episode, social media influencer Lorna Luxe launches her new summer range and has big plans for a glamorous shoot(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane and Arron Crascall go on adventures around the world, using only a smartphone to gain the vital information they need to survive. In the first episode, they cross the Sahara, where they face challenges including making a fire with camel dung, cooking a lizard while it is still alive, and surviving an encounter with a spitting cobra(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

They'll be scouring an antiques fair in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire, with the help of Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell, in a bid to make the most cash - and while they're busy, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the BBC's famous Maida Vale studios(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel continues to blackmail Sharon, leaving Lisa to question what exactly is going on - but Mel refuses to reveal the truth. Stuart goes to drastic lengths to help Rainie who is struggling with withdrawal symptoms. Iqra's treatment by Gray and Ian prompts her to quit her job at the restaurant. Wanda gives Ted another chance and the pair have a heart-to-heart about Joyce(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Matt Allwright and Nikki Fox compare cost and customer service at the nation's biggest supermarkets, and a new Swab Mob investigation reveals hygiene failings at an iconic British institution. An airport parking company taking its customers for a ride gets a surprise of its own in Rogue Traders and Helen Skelton exposes the household staple causing chaos because of the way the packet says we should get rid of it(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate the tragic death of a seven-year-old girl when the bouncy castle she was playing on was swept away by a gust of wind. They also enlist the help of soccer superstar Wayne Rooney as they chase down a couple making a fortune out of fake football memorabilia(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera

programme.desc: Daniel makes a decision about the future, and Ruhma finds herself in a bizarre situation with a pizza delivery man, a porter and a woman who does not want a father for her baby(n)

programme.desc: The team helps a woman living in Salford to recreate the traditional German garden her family had when she was a child, with space divided up for fruit, vegetables and lawn(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth traverse south-west Wales, where they learn about traditional boats and sheep farming before attending an auction in Carmarthen(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal presents more backstage gossip, joined on the sofa by some of this series' dancers. Plus, the weekend's performances are examined in Choreography Corner(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the pressure from Mel rising, Sharon suggests going for lunch with Keanu and Louise to talk but is frustrated when Phil invites himself along. Ben tries to impress Jason, one of Phil's business associates, by telling him he can get him a good deal on a fancy car. Ted and Wanda's second date ends in disaster, Rainie loses control after Ian confronts her about her drug-taking and Gray tells Chantelle he's going to see a therapist about his behaviour(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Evan shows no sign of giving up and pursues Chloe to a cottage in the countryside, where his obsession with her takes a violent turn that leaves her in fear for her life. Cameron receives bad news that forces him to abandon his efforts to help Ric, who has finally been forced to acknowledge his deteriorating health(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Jennifer O'Leary examines the impact of Margaret Thatcher's election on the conflict, hearing a shocking admission about the Brighton bomb which almost killed the prime minister and talking to individuals who were involved in smuggling weapons for the IRA. Plus, how Gerry Adams was elected to parliament led to rising tension between the political and paramilitary wings of the Republican movement(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Rachel finally comes face to face with Shaun, who offers up the location of the CIA safe house before managing to evade arrest once more. The fugitive soldier flees into an encounter with two mysterious strangers, who offer to provide him with the answers he is looking for. Rachel pays Napier a visit, and the meeting leaves her more convinced than ever that the CCTV network has been compromised(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Documentary following six junior doctors beginning new placements at Salford Royal hospital. A doctor assigned to the geriatrics ward has to work on his bedside manner, while a colleague in the colorectal ward has her knowledge of bowel surgery put to the test and is given the difficult task of delivering some bad news to a patient(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth continue to scour the shops of South Wales for antiques, where one expert takes a punt on a piece of local history(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. One team has to master two vegetarian dishes, while the other team takes on a fava bean fallafel with taboulleh and flatbreads. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at The Metropolitan Police Training Academy in Hendon, North London(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue Perkins continues on her trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. In the concluding episode, Sue travels to the ancient city of Kyoto to spend time with geishas and meets those working hard to keep geisha culture alive. She then heads south of Kyoto to experience free diving with Ama divers, before travelling to Hiroshima, where she meets Mr Tetsushi, an 84-year-old survivor of the atomic blast during the Second World War. Finally, Sue returns to Tokyo, to see how the Japanese are tackling challenges of the future(n)

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates with the studio audience and guest panellists(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at life in the In The Style offices in Greater Manchester and the new world of ultra-fast fashion. The series follows the work of 32-year-old business owner Adam Frisby and his team during the summer season, as the company competes with rival fast fashion brands to sign up the latest talent, come up with the hottest styles and supply them faster and better than any other brand. In the first episode, social media influencer Lorna Luxe launches her new summer range and has big plans for a glamorous shoot(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane and Arron Crascall go on adventures around the world, using only a smartphone to gain the vital information they need to survive. In the first episode, they cross the Sahara, where they face challenges including making a fire with camel dung, cooking a lizard while it is still alive, and surviving an encounter with a spitting cobra(n)

Plus, advice on ingrown hairs and the guide to vitamin and mineral supplements covers Omega 3(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh marks the 75th anniversary of D-Day by creating an accessible garden in Derbyshire for Jack Parrott, a veteran approaching his 100th birthday. The team seeks out advice on clever new ways to create raised beds and patio-size greenhouses to find ways of allowing Jack to once again indulge in his life-long love of gardening(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell take people on an emotional journey reuniting them with long-lost family members. Ann Munro, 63, from Ringwood in Hampshire, gave up her son for adoption when she was 21 and her life ever since has been overshadowed by guilt and the longing to ask for his forgiveness. Louise Kendall, a 28-year-old nurse, lives in Bradford with her four-year-old son. She's hoping to meet her French father for the first time as she travels to Dijon, the city where her parents fell in love(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v USA (Kick-off 11.45am). Coverage of the Pool C clash, which takes place at Kobe Misaki Stadium in Kobe City, Japan. The last time these nations faced each other at a World Cup was in France in 2007, and on that occasion England triumphed by a score of 28-10, in what was considered a lacklustre performance. Pundits at the time commented that the side was a shadow of the 2003 World Cup-winning squad, and predicted them to struggle, but they would of course go on to reach the final before succumbing to South Africa. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Sir Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, pitch-side contributions by David Flatman, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's culinary journey brings him to the lakeside town of Annecy, where he wanders around the market to select ingredients, before creating dishes making the most of local tomatoes, red mullet and lobster. programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: Documentary going inside the walls of six prisons to offer a sharp insight into the harsh realities of life behind bars. Thirty-year-old repeat offender Gemma, serving five years for burglary at HMP Downview in Surrey, talks about how her upbringing led her into a life of crime. She has a reputation for bad behaviour - which soon becomes apparent when she threatens a fellow prisoner. Meanwhile, new officer Jon Aldridge begins his first shift at HMP Bullingdon in Oxfordshire and seeks advice from one of the most respected men on the wing - top dog Anthony(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Good Health
programme.desc: Sian Williams examines self-testing kits for heart disease, cholesterol and high blood pressure, Ranj Singh discovers whether stand-up desks can help prevent back pain, and Verity Mann from the Good Housekeeping Institute offers tips on keeping the home free of mould and mildew. Plus, advice on ingrown hairs and the guide to vitamin and mineral supplements covers Omega 3(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh marks the 75th anniversary of D-Day by creating an accessible garden in Derbyshire for Jack Parrott, a veteran approaching his 100th birthday. The team seeks out advice on clever new ways to create raised beds and patio-size greenhouses to find ways of allowing Jack to once again indulge in his life-long love of gardening(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell take people on an emotional journey reuniting them with long-lost family members. Ann Munro, 63, from Ringwood in Hampshire, gave up her son for adoption when she was 21 and her life ever since has been overshadowed by guilt and the longing to ask for his forgiveness. Louise Kendall, a 28-year-old nurse, lives in Bradford with her four-year-old son. She's hoping to meet her French father for the first time as she travels to Dijon, the city where her parents fell in love(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, passenger experience manager Demi deals with a medical emergency. Meanwhile, border force officer Rob returns to cross-examine unsuspecting passengers arriving into the UK(n)

programme.desc: While Cole gets into the holiday spirit for his first Christmas with Maya, Murtaugh hopes he can convince RJ to come back home from Costa Rica for the holidays. programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v USA (Kick-off 11.45am). Coverage of the Pool C clash, which takes place at Kobe Misaki Stadium in Kobe City, Japan. The last time these nations faced each other at a World Cup was in France in 2007, and on that occasion England triumphed by a score of 28-10, in what was considered a lacklustre performance. Pundits at the time commented that the side was a shadow of the 2003 World Cup-winning squad, and predicted them to struggle, but they would of course go on to reach the final before succumbing to South Africa. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Sir Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, pitch-side contributions by David Flatman, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's culinary journey brings him to the lakeside town of Annecy, where he wanders around the market to select ingredients, before creating dishes making the most of local tomatoes, red mullet and lobster. programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: Documentary going inside the walls of six prisons to offer a sharp insight into the harsh realities of life behind bars. programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack is offered the job of bus inspector and Stan is promised extra overtime, for which he will not have to work - as well as a glamorous conductress. Reg Varney and Bob Grant star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Arthur is attracted to Beryl, the clippie on his regular bus. However, she has only two interests in life - men with a fondness for athletics, and those with a good head of hair. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Tired of getting nowhere with Miss Jones, Rigsby switches his amorous attentions to new lodger Marilyn, who turns out to be an exotic dancer - complete with python. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop and Ma become the target of dirty tricks and smear tactics during the election campaign, and Charley is placed in a compromising position by Abigail's teacher(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: There's much excitement in Aidensfield as Lord Ashfordly returns home with his new wife, and the villagers are pleasantly surprised to discover how glamorous and charming she is. However, a series of violent hijackings leaves the newlyweds shaken and in need of protection from Mike - but the intrepid officer's suspicions are aroused by the blushing bride's odd behaviour(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack tells Sarah the insurers will pay for temporary accommodation. Seth prepares to go back to work. Frank is sure that Kim isn't entitled to a penny of his assets(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre feels sorry for Emily having had the shock of Brendan's death. Sally asks Kevin if they can try for another baby. Audrey decides she belongs in Weatherfield(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Colin is prepared to give Liz time to sort herself out. Sally is startled when Joe arrives to collect Jonathan. Raquel has a great time with Ken(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: There's much excitement in Aidensfield as Lord Ashfordly returns home with his new wife, and the villagers are pleasantly surprised to discover how glamorous and charming she is. However, a series of violent hijackings leaves the newlyweds shaken and in need of protection from Mike - but the intrepid officer's suspicions are aroused by the blushing bride's odd behaviour(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Pete, Chris, James and Tim are all interviewed by Cassie and Sunny about the time they spent renting a holiday cottage near where Hayley disappeared over the turn of the millennium. James is the first to be called in, and reveals that Tim and Chris both had their families with them at the time. He recalls seeing press coverage of Hayley's disappearance, but never having seen her in person. Meanwhile, Chris is looking at flats with Jamila, who resolves that she needs to know everything about his past before she will take their relationship further(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: It becomes clear to the detectives that the four men have conspired to come up with a false account of what took place the night Hayley disappeared. Jake is assigned to track down Tim Finch's ex-wife to hear her version of events - and uncovers disturbing information about Chris Lowe's past. On a personal front, Sunny is offered the opportunity to rekindle his past marriage, and Cassie worries about her father's failing memory(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old girl is found dead in her home, Brooks and Devlin cannot be sure a crime was even committed. On further investigation, an unlikely murder weapon is uncovered and a suspect identified - a recently released serial rapist. Determined the accused is guilty, Steel pursues him with every power available - pushing the boundaries of the law to the limit. Crime drama, guest starring Kevin Doyle (Drop Dead Gorgeous), with Ben Daniels(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector bans all the garage staff from smoking on duty - with dangerous consequences. Starring Reg Varney, Doris Hare, Anna Karen and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The season of goodwill descends on the Larkin household, and the family celebrates the 'perfick' Christmas. Drama, based on the characters created by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mike joins forces with Scotland Yard when a gang of thieves turns up in Aidensfield, apparently plotting to steal a valuable horse from local trainer Kieron Doyle. The heroic copper doggedly pursues his quarry to the detriment of his relationship with Tricia - but tragedy strikes, leaving him heartbroken(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Michael is getting worried about his trial. Kim discovers her credit card has been cancelled. Carol receives a phone call from Lorraine's headmistress(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank tells Chris that he is divorcing Kim. Mrs Jeffries suggests Carol speak to a social worker about Lorraine. Zoe tells Archie she has got a job interview in Edinburgh(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy gets four GCSEs, but Ken is disappointed. Kevin is worried about the trial. Angie tells Curly she's trapped and wants out. Sally tells Gail about Joe's declaration(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly agrees to be Reg's best man in the hope Angie will get romantic at the wedding. Amy is thrilled that Andy likes Dominic so much. Des talks to Angie about what she wants(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mike joins forces with Scotland Yard when a gang of thieves turns up in Aidensfield, apparently plotting to steal a valuable horse from local trainer Kieron Doyle. The heroic copper doggedly pursues his quarry to the detriment of his relationship with Tricia - but tragedy strikes, leaving him heartbroken(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of prison officer Charlie Tyner, Brooks and Devlin discover he had been abusing his position and convicts in his charge. A suspect is brought to trial, but Steel and Phillips go in pursuit of a career criminal connected to Tyner and have to persuade their key witness to give evidence against a man she claims to love. Starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy tells Jim there's nothing going on between Liz and Colin. Ken tries to teach Raquel how to go shopping in France. Mike suggests that he pays for Mark to attend private school(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve is furious to find Colin has spent the night with Liz. The Websters enjoy the country life. Jim warns Colin that Liz is still his wife and he won't see her getting hurt(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police leap into action when a labourer at the Kirkbys' farm dies of strychnine poisoning, believing a killer is on the loose. As the finger of suspicion moves from one villager to another, the scene is set for tragedy. Meanwhile, the Ashfordly Rotary Club pensioners head for the seaside town of Whitby - but David's mangy mutt Alfred proves to be something of a liability(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica investigates when an old friend who presents a classical music radio show is accused of murdering a manager who had attempted to modernise the station(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan indulges a whim and lifts Doreen off the ground, but strains his back in the process. Classic comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack asks Arthur's sister out to the Boxing Day social - much to her brother's displeasure. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Gloomy new tenant Mr Gray moves into Rigsby's boarding house, but proves so depressed that he barely seems to notice the dingy surroundings, and keeps talking about 'going to a better place' - raising fears he is contemplating suicide. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and David Swift(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. A seat becomes vacant on the local council when Sam Popham is taken ill, so Pop stands in the election, and Mariette returns from holiday with Charley. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton tries to catch a thief who has been stealing tractors from farms around Aidensfield. Meanwhile, Vernon decides it's time to relieve a few more people of their hard-earned cash, and sets himself up as a horticultural expert and garden designer - but makes the mistake of enlisting David's help(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Michael receives his trial date. Seth interrupts Alan's dinner party with some tragic news. Rachel challenges Mark to a 10-mile fell race(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Seth struggles to accept that Meg has died. Kim receives a letter from Frank's solicitor. Jack and Sarah hear some alarming news at their meeting with the architect(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des asks Colin not to make his relationship with Liz so obvious. Nicky is upset when Brendan accuses him of being lazy. Ken worries that Raquel is getting too involved with him(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Joe tells Sally he's certain he loves her. Colin gets fed up of Des lecturing him. Brendan collapses in the shop in front of Emily, Gail and Ken(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton tries to catch a thief who has been stealing tractors from farms around Aidensfield. Meanwhile, Vernon decides it's time to relieve a few more people of their hard-earned cash, and sets himself up as a horticultural expert and garden designer - but makes the mistake of enlisting David's help(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. A man and woman claiming to be social workers visit a mother and tell her they need to take her son away. However, when they fail to bring him back, it transpires that the pair are not known by the authorities. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. Co-starring Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective now has a murder inquiry on his hands as the body found on the moors is identified as 11-year-old Andre Petri. With Kyle Heath still missing, Banks divides his team to search for a connection between the cases, and when post-mortem examinations show Andre was killed by a heroin overdose, it appears the two boys are linked by the illegal drug trade. Starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star(n)
